title: Chest pain non-acute follow-up
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports documented stable-pain characteristics, risk and prior-investigation context, and clinician-selected or reviewed investigation discussion without automatic testing or diagnosis.
examination.rationale: The NICE stable chest-pain section supports documenting interval symptoms, function, associated features, cardiovascular risk, prior investigations, examination, and clinician-reviewed results without inferring stable angina or another diagnosis.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documented stable-pain characteristics, risk and prior-investigation context, and clinician-selected or reviewed investigation discussion without automatic testing or diagnosis.

source.title: Recent-onset chest pain of suspected cardiac origin: assessment and diagnosis

exact_section.heading: Section 1.3 - stable chest-pain assessment and investigation context

investigations.text: Supports documenting concerns, uncertainty, questions, explanation of reviewed tests, shared decisions, and clinician-provided advice for recurrent pain without generating patient instructions.
investigations.rationale: The information section supports documenting patient questions, clinician explanation of reviewed tests, shared decisions, recurrent-pain safety advice, and arranged follow-up without generating advice.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports documenting concerns, uncertainty, questions, explanation of reviewed tests, shared decisions, and clinician-provided advice for recurrent pain without generating patient instructions.

source_limitations: Current pain status, pattern and triggers, associated features, risk, prior diagnosis and tests, examination, ECG, biomarkers, clinician interpretation, treatment, referral, safety-net content, and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
